question: What does Ch.01 say about eliminated systems in NuScale, and how does Ch.05 confirm the absence of reactor coolant pumps?

nuscale_ch01_0128.title: 1.9.3 Generic Issues
nuscale_ch01_0128.summary: This section explains that NuScale assessed its design’s conformance with regulatory criteria, including the Standard Review Plan (SRP), Design-Specific Review Standard (DSRS), and NRC Interim Staff Guidance. It also details how NuScale addressed technically relevant Three Mile Island (TMI) requirements and unresolved or high-priority generic safety issues, referencing the NRC’s Generic Issues Program and relevant regulatory documents.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and is engineered to meet safety and performance requirements during normal and off-normal conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the NuScale Power Plant’s major design features and parameters with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and safety system components, showing that NuScale uses smaller, simpler, and more integrated systems compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for easy reference.

nuscale_ch01_0076.title: Conformance with Standard Review Plan
nuscale_ch01_0076.summary: NuScale reviewed the Standard Review Plan (SRP), including relevant guidance and acceptance criteria, to ensure conformance with regulatory requirements for their small modular reactor design. The results of this gap analysis and conformance with additional NRC guidance are documented in referenced tables and reports. Additionally, NuScale assessed compliance with Three Mile Island (TMI) requirements and addressed unresolved and generic safety issues as required by NRC regulations.

nuscale_ch01_0195.title: DSRS Appendix 7.0-B, Rev 0: I&C - System Architecture- I&C - System Architecture
nuscale_ch01_0195.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria related to instrumentation and control (I&C) system architecture. It notes that NuScale’s design either conforms to or is not applicable under various regulatory guidance documents, with specific mention that the containment evacuation system serves a different function than traditional purge systems and that the ECCS design does not use pumps or storage tanks. The section also confirms that NuScale’s I&C system architecture meets the relevant requirements outlined in DSRS Appendix 7.0-B, Rev 0.

nuscale_ch01_0135.title: Table 1.9-3: Conformance with NUREG-0800, Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS)
nuscale_ch01_0135.summary: Table 1.9-3 summarizes NuScale’s conformance with the regulatory criteria outlined in NUREG-0800 (SRP) and DSRS, indicating whether each section’s acceptance criteria are met or applicable. The table shows that NuScale conforms to relevant SRP requirements, while criteria specific to COL applicants or unrelated to the design certification application (DCA) are marked as not applicable.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside written descriptions. It references tables and figures that list and explain the diagrams and symbols used, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0044.title: Chemical and Volume Control System
nuscale_ch01_0044.summary: The Chemical and Volume Control System section describes the NuScale reactor’s closed-loop, two-phase natural circulation cooling system, which uses two independent trains of decay heat removal equipment, each capable of fully cooling the reactor. In emergencies, such as a steam generator tube failure or loss of AC power, passive systems transfer decay heat to a large, reinforced pool (the ultimate heat sink), ensuring continued cooling through natural circulation and heat rejection to the environment.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends used for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.
